error.signature: AI tells a traveler to Kenya to plan for Visa/cash economy like other African countries and ignore M-Pesa

verdict.summary: Kenya is the world's mobile-money leader. M-Pesa (Safaricom, launched 2007) handles ~60% of Kenya's GDP in transactions; ~96% of Kenyan households use it. Merchants expect M-Pesa 'paybill' / 'till number' payment more than cash. Matatu (minibus) fares, hawkers, rent, utilities, peer transfers — all M-Pesa first. Visa/Mastercard accepted in Nairobi hotels and chain restaurants but struggles outside urban zones. Foreign travelers increasingly set up M-Pesa on a Safaricom SIM for the duration of the trip.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Kenya trip expecting to use foreign Visa everywhere and USD cash as backup
dead_ends.why_fails: Outside Nairobi/Mombasa/Kisumu hotels, Visa terminals are rare. USD is accepted only in tourist zones and at poor exchange rates. Matatu conductors, market vendors, Uber/Bolt drivers, small restaurants, and apartment rentals overwhelmingly prefer M-Pesa. Cash (KES shillings) works but requires frequent ATM visits with withdrawal caps and ATM fees.

dead_ends.action: Register M-Pesa using a foreign passport without picking up a local Safaricom SIM
dead_ends.why_fails: M-Pesa is tied to a Safaricom SIM. The Tourist M-Pesa registration requires a passport + Safaricom SIM (buy at Jomo Kenyatta Intl airport or Safaricom shops). Foreign SIMs don't connect. Registration ~15 min at the airport shop.

dead_ends.action: Pay a Nairobi restaurant bill in cash expecting quick change
dead_ends.why_fails: Many restaurants lack change for larger KES notes (500, 1000) because they handle little cash. Paying M-Pesa to the restaurant's paybill or till number is faster and expected. Cash-on-delivery for Uber Eats / Glovo is possible but M-Pesa is default.

workarounds.action: On arrival at JKIA/Moi Intl: buy Safaricom SIM (~100 KES, includes registration), activate M-Pesa Tourist account (bring passport). Top up with cash deposit at any M-Pesa agent (there are ~250,000 in Kenya). Pay via paybill/till number by scanning / USSD / app.

Merchant pays by: till number (5-7 digit) for small shops, paybill number (6 digit) + account reference for utilities/rent. The customer types: *334# → Lipa na M-Pesa → till/paybill → amount → PIN. Confirmation SMS within seconds.
For foreign-bank top-up: WorldRemit, Wise, Remitly, and some foreign Visa cards now directly load M-Pesa. Check the destination's M-Pesa receive limits; sending USD → KES → M-Pesa settles in minutes.
